frameworks.name: evidence integration / accumulation
frameworks.one_line: optimal gradual integration of pulsatile noisy evidence for decisions

frameworks.name: degenerate / non-integration strategies
frameworks.one_line: alternative heuristics that approximate near-optimal performance without integration

core_question: Do rats actually integrate pulsatile evidence in the Poisson Clicks task, or use degenerate non-integration strategies?
key_assumptions: Poisson Clicks task is optimally solved by gradual integration
Pulsatile stimulus structure makes strategies behaviorally distinguishable
Behavioral choice patterns identify the underlying decision strategy
manipulations: Comparison of 3 candidate degenerate strategies vs the optimal integration strategy
Aggregation across 515 rats and >35 million trials
key_findings: Non-integration strategies can yield near-optimal performance on accumulation tasks
Pulsatile click structure permits dissociating integration from degenerate strategies
Overwhelmingly, rats are using an integration strategy
interpretations: Behavior supports gradual evidence integration as the mechanism in Poisson Clicks rats
Task design (pulsatile noise) is sufficient to rule out degenerate look-alike strategies
